meaning: n.[建]凸出壁外的窗,凸肚窗
examples: He found her sitting in an oriel window reading Plato's beautiful account of the death of Socrates.
An outside staircase, placed on a partly demolished oriel, leads up to the top of the new flat roof, where a new terrace is created from timber flooring.
"It should work out as a zero sum game, " said Michael Trippit of Oriel Securities.
The party will then attend a reception at the Deanery and lunch as guests of Oriel College.
"I think it will be a similar company, " said Redwan Ahmed, analyst with Oriel Securities in London.
